name: Chocolate-Bottom Mini-Cupcakes
recipeInstructions:
1. In a mixing bowl, beat cream cheese, egg, sugar, and salt until smooth.
2. Stir in chocolate chips; set aside.
3. For the batter, combine water, oil, vinegar, and vanilla extract in another mixing bowl.
4. Combine remaining ingredients (flour, sugar, cocoa, baking soda, and salt) and add to the liquid mixture. Beat well. The batter will be thin.
5. Spoon about 2 tablespoons of batter into greased or paper-lined miniature muffin cups.
6. Top each cup with about 1 teaspoon of filling.
7. Bake at 350°F for 18-23 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in the chocolate portion comes out clean.
8. Cool for 10 minutes; remove to wire racks to cool completely.
